title: Port with same name as ENUMITEM
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

What would you like added/supported?

I have a module that imports a package that contains an enumeration which has an option "INT". The module also has a port named "INT".

Verilator gives an error:
%Error: test.sv:16:16: Unsupported in C: Variable has same name as ENUMITEM 'INT': 'INT'

Commercial simulators accept this construct and AFAIK, it's a matter of scoping rules where the port 'INT' shadows the enumeration item 'INT'.

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

Verilator version: 5.038 on Linux

Can you attach an example that runs on other simulators?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained and self-checking so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Had a look in the source code and some stuff related to the Error reporting in V3LinkDot.cpp and V3SymTable.h, but this seems to be something pretty fundamental related to naming (mangling).
Enum items shadowing other enum items seems to give no errors in the codebase.
